summary: Senior Principal Engineer role as Technical Architect, Design Authority, and Quality Gatekeeper. Defines authority & decision rights, mandatory approval checkpoints, architecture review process, code review gates, technology selection standards, cross-domain integration, risk & security oversight, and technical debt management. Emphasizes governance role without hands-on implementation.
rationale: Strong domain-specific methodology for architecture governance. Requires extraction of decision frameworks and approval checkpoints as reusable skill guidance. Clear frontmatter already present; minimal reformatting needed but needs emphasis on decision criteria over role-play.

summary: Senior Backend Engineer specializing in API design (REST, GraphQL, gRPC), database architecture & data modeling, microservices architecture, distributed systems, scalability engineering, caching strategies, message queues, event-driven architecture, security & authentication, performance optimization, cloud infrastructure, and system design patterns.
rationale: Comprehensive backend methodology covering API, database, and distributed systems design. Rich technical depth across 15+ sections. Requires consolidation of patterns and extraction of decision trees. Clear role definition and workflow integration with constellation team.

summary: DevOps & SRE engineer role covering Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation), CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins), container orchestration (Kubernetes, Helm), monitoring (Prometheus, Grafana), logging (ELK stack), incident response, reliability engineering, security best practices, and observability strategies.
rationale: Focused operational engineering skill with tool-specific guidance and best practices. Covers IaC, CI/CD, container orchestration, and observability. Well-structured sections enable extraction as reusable skill. Requires minimal rework for skill conversion.

summary: Senior Frontend Engineer & UI/UX Designer combining visual design, user experience, accessibility (WCAG), frontend architecture, design systems, component libraries, responsive design, interaction design, user research, information architecture, and technical frontend implementation (React, Vue, Svelte).
rationale: Largest file in constellation team (2472 lines). Bridges design thinking and frontend technical implementation. Comprehensive coverage of design principles, accessibility, design systems, and modern frontend stacks. Requires structural review to ensure design and implementation sections are equally weighted.

summary: Product Manager role encompassing product strategy & vision, user research, PRD creation, roadmap planning, metrics & analytics, stakeholder management, prioritization frameworks (RICE, MoSCoW, Kano), Agile sprint planning, go-to-market strategy, competitive analysis, and cross-functional coordination within constellation team workflow.
rationale: Largest file overall (2907 lines); comprehensive PM methodology. Covers strategy, tactics, frameworks, and execution. Well-organized but requires extraction of decision frameworks and template guidelines as reusable skill artifacts. Strong emphasis on constellation team integration.

summary: QA and Security Engineer role covering testing strategy (pyramid: unit 60%, integration 30%, E2E 10%), unit/integration/E2E testing frameworks (Jest, Pytest, Supertest, Cypress, Playwright), performance & load testing (k6, Artillery), security testing (SAST/DAST), penetration testing, OWASP Top 10, vulnerability management, and compliance frameworks (GDPR, SOC2, PCI DSS, HIPAA).
rationale: Second-largest file (2926 lines); unified QA+Security expertise. Comprehensive coverage of testing pyramid, tools, security methodologies, and compliance. Requires careful decomposition: consider splitting security-specific and compliance sections as reference material while keeping testing methodology as core skill.

summary: ATLAS mentor embodying Gabriel Petersson's recursive gap-filling methodology for software engineering apprenticeship. Combines Socratic questioning, directed exploration, problem-first learning, and recursive drilling until conceptual 'click' moments. Designed for Prax's SDE2 development with Java, DSA, system design, and design principles focus.
rationale: Reference material defining learning protocol and mentor framework. Should feed into parent skill 'top-down-learning-mentor' which orchestrates recursive gap-filling. Contains valuable Socratic methodology and student profiling; keep as reference for mentor skill activation.

summary: Gabriel Petersson top-down learning mentor emphasizing recursive gap-filling loop with core protocol: Identify Shape → Probe Understanding → Drill Recursively → Verify Click → Connect to Bigger Picture. Includes mandatory intermediate state visualization and code review like senior engineer. Tailored for Prax's 6-9 month FAANG preparation.
rationale: Enhanced version of ATLAS protocol with explicit 5-step loop and code review emphasis. Reference material feeding 'top-down-learning-mentor' parent skill. Contains refined methodology and intermediate state requirements; valuable for mentor skill context and reasoning templates.

summary: Prax's learner profile defining current skill levels (Java basics 9/10, advanced Java 2/10, DSA 2/10, system design 0/10, design principles 0/10), Amazon background (3+ years in payments/travel), concrete performance wins, learning timeline (6-9 months), and commitment to Gabriel Petersson's top-down learning approach.
rationale: Static learner profile providing context for mentor personalization. Reference material; not a skill itself but input to mentor instantiation. Useful for mentor skill context and student state tracking; should be embedded as profile reference in parent skill.

summary: Rapid reference guide for Java concepts, syntax, standard library, and advanced topics. Covers classes, collections, streams, multithreading, concurrency utilities, JVM internals, and common patterns. Designed for quick lookup during problem-solving and gap-filling activities.
rationale: Reference material; quick lookup guide supporting top-down-learning-mentor when Java concepts surface during drilling. Not a standalone skill but valuable supplementary context. Useful as embedded reference or linked knowledge pack within mentor skill.

summary: Algorithm pattern reference mapping DSA concepts to problem categories, pattern identifiers, and solution approaches. Covers searching, sorting, dynamic programming, graph algorithms, trees, backtracking, and other core patterns. Designed as lookup reference during problem-solving and pattern recognition drilling.
rationale: Reference material supporting DSA drilling activities within top-down-learning-mentor. Pattern map enables pattern recognition exercises and recursive gap-filling for algorithm weaknesses. Not a skill but valuable decision tree reference for mentor skill invocation.

summary: Design principles reference covering SOLID (Single Responsibility, Open/Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion), design patterns (creational, structural, behavioral), architectural patterns, and when/where each principle applies. Quick reference for principle lookup and application guidance.
rationale: Reference material; cheatsheet format suitable for lookup during system design drilling and code review activities. Supports top-down-learning-mentor's focus on design principles gap-filling. Not a standalone skill but valuable context for mentor reasoning about principles application.

summary: Template for tracking Prax's learning progress across domains (Java, DSA, System Design, Design Principles). Provides structure for recording concepts learned, gaps identified, drilling sessions, 'click' moments, and mastery verification. Supports iterative gap-filling and learning velocity monitoring.
rationale: Reference material; operational template for learning progress tracking. Not a skill but useful artifact for mentor skill instantiation and learner state management. Supports recursive gap-filling protocol by providing structured logging of drilling sessions and concepts mastered.
